title: T-014 — Seed fefo_strategy_v1 DSL rule
prompt:
# T-014 — Seed fefo_strategy_v1 DSL rule

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §9.1, §9.2, ADR-029.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Seed the rule registry with `fefo_strategy_v1` whose query implementation matches §9.2 SQL and respects qa_status gating.

## Implementation contract
Seed JSON `packages/db/seed/rules/fefo-strategy-v1.json` declaring inputs (product_id, warehouse_id, qty_required, target_qa_status default ['PASSED','RELEASED']) and outputs (sorted suggestions ranked by expiry asc nulls last, then created_at asc). Implementation hint references §9.2 SQL. Seed script upserts.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/seed/rules/fefo-strategy-v1.json [create]
- packages/db/seed/rules/fefo-strategy-v1.seed.ts [create]
- packages/db/seed/rules/__tests__/fefo-strategy-v1.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the seed runs, when rule_registry is queried, then `fefo_strategy_v1` v1.0 exists with PRD §9.1 input/output shape.
2. Given a sample product fixture with three LPs (expiring 2026-05-01, 2026-05-10, NULL), when the rule executes, then suggestions rank LP-2026-05-01 first and NULL-expiry last.
3. Given an LP with qa_status='HOLD', when the rule executes with default target_qa_status, then the LP is excluded from suggestions.

## Test strategy
- RED: fefo-strategy-v1.test.ts seeds three LP fixtures and asserts ranking and HOLD exclusion.
- Run `pnpm --filter db test fefo-strategy-v1`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not allow blocked or expired LPs.
- Do not change PRD ordering rules.
- Rule must be admin read-only.
